title: Coussarea graciliflora
taxonomy: Coussarea graciliflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. It belongs to the genus Coussarea, which is a group of flowering plants found primarily in tropical and subtropical regions. The species Coussarea graciliflora is native to Central and South America, and is characterized by its slender flowers and delicate foliage. The plant is a member of the Rubiaceae family, which also includes the genus Psychotria, among others. As a member of the Rubiaceae family, Coussarea graciliflora has a complex and diverse classification within the plant kingdom.
